items.claim: Mario Bautista is effective at analyzing and adapting to his opponent's patterns during a fight.
items.quote: He is good at downloading data and adjusting as the fight goes on.

items.claim: Mario Bautista has improved his skill set since his UFC debut against Cory Sandhagen.
items.quote: Bautista has leveled up since his UFC debut against Cory Sandhagen.

items.claim: Mario Bautista is capable of controlling opponents against the cage to drain time.
items.quote: He can hold you against the cage and burn minutes on the clock.

items.claim: Cory Sandhagen is susceptible to being controlled on the ground.
Mario Bautista has developed the ability to defend submission attempts.
items.quote: Bautista knows how to defend sub attempts now.

items.claim: Benoît Saint Denis is highly hittable due to his aggressive, high-pressure style.
items.quote: BSD's very hittable, all gas, no brakes, telegraphed in almost everything that he does.

items.claim: Benoît Saint Denis is vulnerable to body kicks and teep kicks.
items.quote: He can be very open to getting hit with that teeth or body kick.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has not competed in over five years.
items.quote: Conor hasn't fought in over 5 years.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has never recorded a finish in the third, fourth, or fifth round.
items.quote: Conor does not have a single finish in the third, fourth, or fifth round.

items.claim: Max Holloway increases his output and effectiveness as the fight progresses.
items.quote: Max has volume and a snowball effect that builds and builds and builds as the fight goes on.
